text: le ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa bi pu, le ki bui ke, “Maa, ki pɔr, aan ki gbiin kitiŋ kee pu, ki li lik kitiŋ ngbaan. Li kpa mpɔɔn ijan, ni inyoon, ni tiwan nimɔk fu ki baar kitiŋ pu na.”
translated: I will tell you a story that happened, I will say that, "Mother, I went, I did not find the kitiŋ fruit, I saw a big kitiŋ tree. It had three branches, one was bent, and the other two, I climbed the kitiŋ tree on them."

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Tɔ, m ga puu tipuur tii si. Aa ni aapuu, ni saabim, ni bipuutiib koo buŋɔb ngbaan ni.
translated: My father has a large farm. He grows yams, corn, and other crops there.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Cha m ban ni nnyun nan tii nimi, aan ni finn nitaa man; kal busub bue taab, ki fuur man.
translated: I am not a child anymore, I am a grown man; I can handle my own affairs, so leave me alone.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Baah fuu ni na le u bui bi ke, “M bee ke nite aa ki gee maabɔr ke waah nan gee mi njan pu na; tɔ, nte Aawumbɔr bi m chee.
translated: When I was in the world, I said, "I will do something and become rich and build a house and live in it; but, it is God who knows.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”
translated: And he said to him, “Go, see if it is well with your brother Jacob and the flock.” Then Jacob said, “The word of God is not a word of death that will kill me, nor a word of war that will fight me.”

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: le ki ŋa tinyoor ŋa bi pu, le ki bui ke, “Maa, ki pɔr, aan ki gbiin kitiŋ kee pu, ki li lik kitiŋ ngbaan. Li kpa mpɔɔn ijan, ni inyoon, ni tiwan nimɔk fu ki baar kitiŋ pu na.”
translated: I will tell you a story, listen to me, “Mother, wait, let me go and bring the kitiŋ, I will go and collect the kitiŋ fruit. It is for the funeral of my father, my mother, and all my siblings that I am going to bring the kitiŋ.”

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Tɔ, m ga puu tipuur tii si. Aa ni aapuu, ni saabim, ni bipuutiib koo buŋɔb ngbaan ni.
translated: My father, I am going to the farm today. I will be with my sister, my friend, and some children from the neighborhood.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Cha m ban ni nnyun nan tii nimi, aan ni finn nitaa man; kal busub bue taab, ki fuur man.
translated: If you do not listen to my words, you will see something; a new thing is about to happen, and it will shock you.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.

translated: I told them that I would not go with them to the farm because I was sick, but they did not believe me.

text: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”
translated: God said to him, “Your name is Jacob, but you will no longer be called Jacob; your name will be Israel.” So he named him Israel.
